item.title: Artifacts in Claude Code have been life changing. Excited to expand to Pro and Max!
item.description: Artifacts in Claude Code have been life changing. Excited to expand to Pro and Max!

item.title:
In the next version of Claude Code: subagents run in the background by default, so you can keep talking to Claude while your subagents work
If you want your agent to run in the foreground, just tell
item.description:
In the next version of Claude Code: subagents run in the background by default, so you can keep talking to Claude while your subagents work
If you want your agent to run in the foreground, just tell Claude

item.title: As engineering, product, design, DS, etc. melt into a new kind of role, I was reflecting on what roles might look like in the future. For example, when I look at the Claude Code team I see what I thin
item.description:
As engineering, product, design, DS, etc. melt into a new kind of role, I was reflecting on what roles might look like in the future. For example, when I look at the Claude Code team I see what I think is five archetypes:
1. Prototyper: comes up with brand new ideas; churns out many ideas, most of

item.title:
We're launching Claude Tag today. Tag Claude into Slack and it works in channel with you. It’s proactive, multiplayer, with its own identity and memory.
But it’s not just a bot in Slack. Over the las
item.description:
We're launching Claude Tag today. Tag Claude into Slack and it works in channel with you. It’s proactive, multiplayer, with its own identity and memory.
But it’s not just a bot in Slack. Over the last few months, it’s totally changed how we use Claude

item.title:
Cool way to use Claude Code: deciphering Linear A, a 3500 year old written language from Crete
https://t.co/Aqd4ZG7Cum
Hope this holds up in peer review! 🤞
item.description:
Cool way to use Claude Code: deciphering Linear A, a 3500 year old written language from Crete
https://t.co/Aqd4ZG7Cum
Hope this holds up in peer review! 🤞

item.title: I've been using Artifacts in Claude Code for everything: visual explanations of tricky code, system diagrams, quick previews of a few animation options, data analyses and dashboards I share with the t
item.description: I've been using Artifacts in Claude Code for everything: visual explanations of tricky code, system diagrams, quick previews of a few animation options, data analyses and dashboards I share with the team. They are a game changer for how I work with Claude. Can't wait to hear what you think!

item.description: Enjoy!

item.title: Fable 5 is the biggest step up I’ve felt in our models since Opus 4.5 back in November. After 4.5 came out I uninstalled my IDE when I realized that I’d been doing 100% of my coding in a terminal for
item.description: Fable 5 is the biggest step up I’ve felt in our models since Opus 4.5 back in November. After 4.5 came out I uninstalled my IDE when I realized that I’d been doing 100% of my coding in a terminal for a few weeks. With Fable, it’s felt like Claude has stepped up from being a coding agent to a thought

item.title: We talk a lot about how important it is to set up self-verification loops. Especially in the age of powerful models that can run for long periods of time, self-verification is a key ingredient that en
item.description: We talk a lot about how important it is to set up self-verification loops. Especially in the age of powerful models that can run for long periods of time, self-verification is a key ingredient that enables the model to run for much longer, delivering a result that is closer to what you intended, so

item.title: RT @karpathy: This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The bench…

item.title:
Fable 5 is now available in Claude Code and Cowork
Fable is the best model I have used for coding, by a wide margin. It is a big step up, enabling less prompts and steers, more efficient token use, b
item.description:
Fable 5 is now available in Claude Code and Cowork
Fable is the best model I have used for coding, by a wide margin. It is a big step up, enabling less prompts and steers, more efficient token use, better code quality, better tool use, more intelligent self-verification, longer running sessions, an

item.title:
Just landed nested subagent support in Claude Code
Starting to experiment more with agents kicking off agents as a way to better manage context. Capped at depth=5 to start, going out in today’s relea
item.description:
Just landed nested subagent support in Claude Code
Starting to experiment more with agents kicking off agents as a way to better manage context. Capped at depth=5 to start, going out in today’s release.
Lmk what you think!

item.title:
When we first demoed Claude Code internally, it got two reactions on Slack.
A year after GA, @_catwu and I sat down to talk about what's changed: why I use auto mode instead of plan mode, how routin
item.description:
When we first demoed Claude Code internally, it got two reactions on Slack.
A year after GA, @_catwu and I sat down to talk about what's changed: why I use auto mode instead of plan mode, how routines fix bugs before I see them, why I do most of my coding from my phone now, and where the product i

item.title:
Seeing a number of benchmarks showing Opus is the best model for long-running work.
Five tips for running Opus autonomously for hours/days:
1. Use auto mode for permissions, so Claude doesn’t ask fo
item.description:
Seeing a number of benchmarks showing Opus is the best model for long-running work.
Five tips for running Opus autonomously for hours/days:
1. Use auto mode for permissions, so Claude doesn’t ask for approval 2. Use dynamic workflows, to have Claude orchestrate hundreds/thousands of agents to get

item.description: RT @AnthropicAI: Our internal data shows Claude is accelerating AI development—a possible path to recursive self-improvement, or AI autonom…

item.title: We doubled Claude Cowork usage limits for the next month. This applies to your 5-hr rate limits. If you’ve been saving up a big messy project, now’s the time.
item.description: We doubled Claude Cowork usage limits for the next month. This applies to your 5-hr rate limits. If you’ve been saving up a big messy project, now’s the time.

item.title:
Salesforce published a detailed writeup on going agentic with Claude Code. A couple things jumped out.
A migration they'd scoped at 231 days shipped in 13. One PR delivered 21 endpoints at 100% test
item.description:
Salesforce published a detailed writeup on going agentic with Claude Code. A couple things jumped out.
A migration they'd scoped at 231 days shipped in 13. One PR delivered 21 endpoints at 100% test coverage.

item.title: Claude Opus 4.8 is out today. It's our strongest coding model yet: up on SWE-bench Pro (from 64.3 to 69.2) and noticeably more honest about its own work. It tells you when it's unsure and catches its
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8 is out today. It's our strongest coding model yet: up on SWE-bench Pro (from 64.3 to 69.2) and noticeably more honest about its own work. It tells you when it's unsure and catches its own bugs instead of declaring victory early. Same price as 4.7.

item.title: > … [W]e keep finding things that are mysterious, even unsettling. We find structures that mirror results from human neuroscience. We find evidence of introspection. We find internal states that funct
item.description: > … [W]e keep finding things that are mysterious, even unsettling. We find structures that mirror results from human neuroscience. We find evidence of introspection. We find internal states that functionally mirror joy, satisfaction, fear, grief, and unease. I don’t know what that means, but I think

item.title:
People often ask what my biggest tip is for getting the most out of Claude Code.
These days my #1 tip is: use auto mode
Auto mode means no more permission prompts. It is the key building block for m
item.description:
People often ask what my biggest tip is for getting the most out of Claude Code.
These days my #1 tip is: use auto mode
Auto mode means no more permission prompts. It is the key building block for multi-clauding: start a session, then while it runs, work on another session in parallel.

item.title:
Big fan of teaching more people the basics of using Claude Code in an accessible way.
So much of the world has not yet used agents. There's a lot of opportunity to level the playing field and expand
item.description:
Big fan of teaching more people the basics of using Claude Code in an accessible way.
So much of the world has not yet used agents. There's a lot of opportunity to level the playing field and expand access.
